summary:
The patient suffered severe facial burns requiring immediate medical attention; however, absence of burned hair indicates an exception among typical symptoms seen with sulfuric acid burns such as blisters and ulcers. Searing pain might be expected but was not reported here, further suggesting this scenario does not align fully with classic cases of sulfuric acid injury leading us to conclude option C stands out as incorrect based provided context clues regarding common manifestations post-exposure.
